narrative.headline: 2022 Nissan Maxima: only a handful of complaints, but two involve serious outcomes
narrative.summary: Owners filed just 5 complaints with NHTSA, spread evenly across five areas (20% each): service brakes, hydraulic, structure, exterior lighting, and engine. No deaths are reported. Two reports stand out by outcome — a brake/hydraulic report tied to 1 crash and 2 injuries, and an engine report involving 1 fire. The low total means there isn't a clear standout problem, but the brakes and engine reports are worth a close look.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: 1 report (20%), but it involved 1 crash and 2 injuries — one of the more serious outcomes here. Test the brakes carefully on a drive.

issues.title: Hydraulic
issues.blurb: 1 report (20%), tied to 1 crash and 2 injuries. It's filed alongside the brake complaint, so check the braking system thoroughly.

issues.title: Structure
issues.blurb: 1 report (20%), with no crash, fire, or injury reported. A minor, isolated complaint.

issues.blurb: 1 report (20%), with no crash, fire, or injury reported. The owner described an uneven headlight beam at night.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: 1 report (20%), but it involved a fire. The owner noticed a burning smell and smoke from the hood, so this one is worth a careful inspection.
